Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The True Shape of Io's Steeple Mountain 97 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Category Theory Illustrated – Types 37 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. If you're running OpenClaw, you probably got hacked in the last week 24 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Vector Meson Dominance 50 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. A Recipe for Steganogravy 78 upvotes, 12 comments
  6. Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac 502 upvotes, 115 comments
  7. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1673 upvotes, 445 comments
  8. Intel Assured Supply Chain Product Brief 38 upvotes, 5 comments
  9. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 58 upvotes, 13 comments
  10. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 355 upvotes, 119 comments
  11. C89cc.sh – standalone C89/ELF64 compiler in pure portable shell 175 upvotes, 56 comments
  12. What Category Theory Teaches Us About DataFrames 125 upvotes, 40 comments
  13. NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns 230 upvotes, 87 comments
  14. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 1057 upvotes, 497 comments
  15. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 199 upvotes, 85 comments
  16. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 106 upvotes, 41 comments
  17. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 311 upvotes, 148 comments
  18. Tailscale's new macOS home 528 upvotes, 271 comments
  19. Show HN: TurboQuant for vector search – 2-4 bit compression 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  20. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 331 upvotes, 169 comments
  21. Samsung Magician disk utility takes 18 steps and two reboots to uninstall 197 upvotes, 103 comments
  22. ESP32-S31: Dual-Core RISC-V SoC with Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, and Advanced HMI 150 upvotes, 81 comments
  23. Critics say EU risks ceding control of its tech laws under U.S. pressure 168 upvotes, 98 comments
  24. Bun: cgroup-aware AvailableParallelism / HardwareConcurrency on Linux 29 upvotes, 12 comments
  25. Cursor 3 504 upvotes, 370 comments
  26. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 49 upvotes, 28 comments
  27. Mercurial Dyson – a plan for the disassembly of planet Mercury 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. TDF ejects its core developers 101 upvotes, 75 comments
  29. Marc Andreessen is wrong about introspection 281 upvotes, 251 comments
  30. Solar and batteries can power the world 174 upvotes, 252 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 4 April 2026. Source here.
